log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable, likely a spreader, packed with UPX and containing an overlay. It was run in multiple sandboxes, including CAPA and VirusTotal Cuckoofork.
The sample dropped 106 files, had 11 unique IPs and 19 unique domains in network activity, and created 1 process. It also has 1 registry persistence key.
The sample created the <PATH_SAMPLE.EXE> process and loaded several system modules, including network and DNS-related DLLs.
The sample sent UDP traffic to 11 different IP addresses on port 137, and performed DNS lookups on 19 different reverse DNS domains, but did not resolve any IP addresses.
The sample created a persistence key in the registry at <HKLM>\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\mssrv32, which points to %WINDIR%\mssrv.exe.
The sample dropped 30 files in various directories, including %APPDATA%, %PROGRAMDATA%, %PROGRAMFILES%, %WINDIR%, and %TEMP%. These files are all PE_EXE files and have unique SHA256 hashes.
